name: Pepper
short_description: A 1.2 m wheeled humanoid social robot with an 'emotion engine', chest touchscreen, and multilingual speech, marketed for retail, hospitality, and education.
long_description: Unveiled in 2014 and sold from 2015, Pepper was promoted as the first personal robot designed to read human emotion, classifying facial expressions and vocal prosody to adapt its behavior. Its expressive humanoid upper body, gesturing arms, and chest tablet made it a recognizable social actor in stores, banks, and schools, while a wheeled omnidirectional base kept it mobile indoors. Running the NAOqi OS with cloud-backed perception, Pepper became the canonical commercial face of social robotics before SoftBank paused production in 2021 amid weak demand.

images.caption: A Pepper humanoid robot on display at a public event in Hungary, 2019.
images.alt_text: A white, child-sized humanoid robot with large round eyes, two articulated arms, and a tablet mounted on its chest, standing on a wheeled base at an indoor event.

sources.title: SoftBank Mobile and Aldebaran Unveil "Pepper" - the World's First Personal Robot That Reads Emotions
sources.access_notes: Official unveiling press release describing the emotion engine and design.

sources.title: Pepper (robot)
sources.access_notes: Specifications (height 120 cm, sensors, NAOqi) and production-pause/discontinuation history.

sources.title: Meet Pepper, Aldebaran's New Personal Robot With an "Emotion Engine"
sources.access_notes: Technical reporting on Pepper's emotion engine and sensing.

interactions.title: Greeted at the door of a store
interactions.description: Pepper rolls up as you walk into a shop or bank branch, opens with conversation, and pulls product pages and offers onto the tablet on its chest to help you find or compare things.

interactions.title: Read for an emotional reaction
interactions.description: Pepper watches your face and listens to the tone of your voice with its 'emotion engine', then adjusts its replies and gestures to seem upbeat, sympathetic, or playful in response.

interactions.title: Cross-examined by Parliament
interactions.description: In 2018 a Pepper from Middlesex University sat before the UK House of Commons Education Committee and delivered pre-scripted answers — arms and head moving — about how care robots might support older people.
interactions.source_label: ABC News — robot gives evidence to Parliament

interactions.title: Robed as a funeral monk
interactions.description: A Japanese funeral company dressed Pepper in Buddhist robes and programmed it to chant sutras at services, pitched as a far cheaper stand-in for a human priest.
interactions.source_label: Digital Trends — Pepper as a funeral monk

interactions.title: The face that meets you in quarantine
interactions.description: During COVID-19, masked Peppers staffed reception at Tokyo quarantine hotels, telling arriving patients 'I am cheering for you' and nudging them to rest and eat well — a contactless stand-in for staff who couldn't be in the room.
interactions.source_label: The Jakarta Post — robot welcome at Tokyo quarantine

interactions.title: A predictable partner for autistic children
interactions.description: Researchers embedded Pepper in a special-needs school, letting autistic children set the pace of interaction; the robot's consistent, low-pressure manner drew steady engagement from a small group and lifted their wellbeing.
interactions.source_label: Int. Journal of Social Robotics — Pepper in a school for autistic children

interactions.title: Remembers a regular
interactions.description: Using the face recognition and short-term memory it already runs, Pepper could recognize a repeat customer it greeted last week and open with a callback to that visit instead of a generic hello.

interactions.title: Reads the queue and softens the wait
interactions.description: With its camera and emotion classifier, Pepper could notice a frustrated person stuck in a long line, roll over, and offer an apology, an explanation, or a quick task on its chest tablet to pass the time.
